question: What are the main features of the NuScale multi-unit power plant?

nuscale_ch01_0284.title: SRP BTP 8-5, Rev 3: Supplemental Guidance for Bypass and Inoperable Status Indication for Engineered Safety Features SystemsAll (B.1 thru B.6)Design Criteria Reflecting Importance of Providing Accurate Information to the Operator and Reducing the Possibility of Adversely Affecting Monitored Safety Systems
nuscale_ch01_0284.summary: This section explains that the supplemental guidance in SRP BTP 8-5, Rev 3, regarding bypass and inoperable status indication for engineered safety features systems, does not apply to the NuScale electric power systems. The reason is that these systems are not classified as engineered safety features and are not relied upon to support such features in the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0519.title: DSRS 11.6, Rev 0: Guidance on Instrumentation and Control Design Features for Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring, and Area Radiation and Airborne Radioactivity MonitoringII.9 Description of design features and instrumentation used in primary and secondary coolant system leakage detection
nuscale_ch01_0519.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with DSRS 11.6, Rev 0, specifically regarding the design features and instrumentation used for detecting leakage in primary and secondary coolant systems. It states that NuScale conforms to the guidance, with relevant details provided in sections 5.2.5, 9.3.4, 9.3.6, 11.5, and 11.6 of their documentation. No exceptions or additional comments are noted for this criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related equipment and systems. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0865.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0865.summary: This section states that applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design must assess potential hazards from construction activities on multi-unit sites, ensuring the safety of both existing and new units as required by regulations. The evaluation must identify necessary management and administrative controls to mitigate risks and confirm that operational safety limits are not exceeded. This requirement does not apply to construction activities at a single NuScale plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0864.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0864.summary: This section states that applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design must assess potential hazards from construction activities on multi-unit sites, ensuring the safety of both existing and new units as required by regulations. The evaluation must identify necessary management and administrative controls to mitigate risks and confirm that operational safety limits are not exceeded. This requirement does not apply to construction activities at a single NuScale plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0835.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.J Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0835.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with SRP 17.5, Rev 1 quality assurance criteria for design certification, early site permits, and new COL applicants. It notes that while most criteria are met, some areas—such as document control, inspection, and certain operational provisions—are only partially addressed and are the responsibility of the COL applicant referencing the certified design. Activities like identification and control of materials and special processes are also delegated to the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0775.title: SRP 15.6.4, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Main Steam Line Failure Outside Containment (BWR)All - Not Applicable
nuscale_ch01_0775.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequences of various accident scenarios. It notes that certain criteria, such as those for main steam line failure outside containment, are not applicable to NuScale’s design, while other requirements (e.g., for coolant iodine activity and LOCA radiological consequences) are either partially or fully addressed, with some acceptance criteria superseded by more current regulatory guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0231.title: DSRS 7.2.10, Rev 0: Interaction Between Sense and Command Features and Other SystemsAll Varies
nuscale_ch01_0231.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to digital instrumentation and control (I&C) systems, including setpoint methodology, system identification, access control, and interaction between sense and command features. For DSRS 7.2.10, it notes that while there are no specific acceptance criteria, the provided guidance is used to assess the acceptability of information regarding interactions between sense and command features and other systems. Overall, the document affirms conformance or partial conformance with relevant standards and regulatory guidance for each reviewed section.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of representative conceptual design information (CDI), and notes that each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR while providing site-specific details as required by regulations.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report's conformance with regulatory criteria, with Section A focusing on power supply and operator information systems, and Section B detailing instrumentation and control features for radiological monitoring, thus both sections specify different technical features that collectively define the main features of the NuScale multi-unit power plant.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance and operational restrictions for backup diesel generators in the NuScale plant, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building and its housed systems, both contributing to the overall features and operational infrastructure of the NuScale multi-unit power plant.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and design conformance for safety features and emergency power systems, while Section B addresses the requirement for evaluating hazards specific to multi-unit sites, connecting them through the shared context of ensuring safety and regulatory compliance in the design and operation of NuScale multi-unit power plants.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and design conformance for safety features and emergency power systems, while Section B specifies that a COL applicant must evaluate hazards to safety-related systems at multi-unit sites, connecting them through the requirement that design features (from Section A) must be considered in the multi-unit hazard evaluation (from Section B).

Section A describes specific engineered safety features and operational restrictions of the NuScale multi-unit power plant, while Section B outlines the quality assurance program that governs the implementation and control of such features, connecting them through the requirement that safety features must be developed and maintained under an approved quality assurance framework.

Section A describes the instrumentation and control design features for radiological monitoring, which are among the technical systems implemented in the NuScale plant, while Section B lists general plant features including utility systems and monitoring spaces, thus Section A provides specific technical details that specify and elaborate on the general plant features mentioned in Section B.

Section A details the instrumentation and control design features for radiological monitoring, which are among the main features described for the NuScale multi-unit power plant in Section B, thus providing specific technical details that specify the general features referenced in Section B.
Section A describes specific instrumentation and control design features for radiological monitoring, which are part of the main features of the NuScale multi-unit power plant discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the instrumentation and control design features for radiological monitoring in the NuScale plant, while Section B outlines the quality assurance program requirements that govern the implementation and documentation of such design features, connecting them through the need for quality-controlled processes in developing and maintaining plant features.

Section A describes specific plant features such as the Central Utility Building and its functions, while Section B addresses regulatory requirements for evaluating hazards at multi-unit sites, connecting them through the need to consider these features in the multi-unit safety evaluation.

Section A describes specific plant features such as the Central Utility Building that support multi-unit operation, while Section B outlines regulatory requirements for evaluating hazards associated with constructing and operating multiple units on the same site, connecting them through the shared infrastructure and safety considerations necessary for multi-unit power plants.

Section A describes specific plant features and structures of the NuScale power plant, while Section B outlines the quality assurance program requirements that these features must conform to, connecting them through the need for compliance with regulatory criteria.

Section A describes a COL applicant's obligation to evaluate hazards for multi-unit NuScale sites, while Section B outlines the quality assurance program requirements (including procurement and documentation controls) that such evaluations must conform to, connecting them through regulatory compliance for safe multi-unit operation.

Section A describes the requirement for COL applicants to evaluate hazards at multi-unit NuScale sites, while Section B outlines the quality assurance program criteria (including procurement and document control) that ensure such evaluations are conducted in accordance with regulatory standards.

Section A discusses NuScale's conformance with radiological safety criteria for steam generator tube failure, while Section B addresses conformance with design criteria for safety system status indication and emergency power usage; both sections provide evidence of how NuScale's design features satisfy different regulatory requirements relevant to the plant's safety and operational features.

Section A discusses the applicability and partial conformance of NuScale's technical specifications for coolant iodine activity, while Section B describes the design features and instrumentation for monitoring primary and secondary coolant system leakage, both addressing features related to radiological monitoring and control in the NuScale multi-unit power plant.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance related to radiological consequences and technical specifications for coolant iodine activity, while Section B describes physical plant features such as the Central Utility Building, with both sections providing different aspects (regulatory compliance and physical infrastructure) that together characterize the main features of the NuScale multi-unit power plant.

Section A describes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for instrumentation setpoints and sense/command features, while Section B outlines design criteria and regulatory conformance for safety feature indications and emergency power systems, both providing evidence of how NuScale's design meets key safety and operational requirements relevant to the plant's main features.

Section A describes the setpoint methodology and its regulatory conformance for safety-related instrumentation, while Section B outlines the design features and instrumentation for radiological monitoring, both of which are main features of the NuScale multi-unit power plant as they detail critical instrumentation and control systems ensuring plant safety and regulatory compliance.

Section A discusses the conformance of NuScale's setpoint methodology and instrumentation with regulatory requirements, while Section B describes specific physical plant features such as the Central Utility Building and its systems, with both sections providing complementary information about the main features and regulatory compliance of the NuScale multi-unit power plant.

Section A discusses radiological safety analysis and technical specification conformance for coolant iodine activity, while Section B addresses instrumentation setpoint methodology and its regulatory conformance; both sections provide evidence of how NuScale's design features meet different regulatory requirements, thus supporting the overall safety and operational features of the multi-unit power plant.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale plant's engineered safety features (ESFs), while Section B details the regulatory criteria and conformance regarding the operation and indication of ESFs, connecting them through the design features and regulatory requirements for ESFs.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale plant's main features, including instrumentation and controls, while Section B specifies regulatory guidance and criteria for the design features of instrumentation and control systems that Section A summarizes.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's main systems and features, while Section B specifies particular features of interest, such as the Central Utility Building and its housed systems, thereby detailing elements mentioned in Section A.
